prompt: sitemap.py is the little tree printer our docs pipeline runs over content/ before every build, and the writers have started drowning in it — a full dump of a big site is hundreds of lines of images and drafts nobody asked about. I want filtering: tree_lines() grows three keyword args, include=None, exclude=None, max_depth=None, and main() grows matching CLI flags (repeatable --include and --exclude, plus --max-depth N). Semantics we agreed on in the team channel: include patterns are fnmatch-style globs matched against FILE basenames only — when any are given, only matching files show up, and a directory only earns its line if at least one visible file survives somewhere beneath it (no more chains of empty folders). exclude patterns match basenames of files AND directories; an excluded directory isn't even walked, and exclude always beats include. Passing empty lists is the same as passing nothing — no filter, not match-nothing. max_depth counts direct children of the root as depth 1; entries deeper than the cap are dropped, a directory sitting at the cap still prints (just without contents), and anything less than 1 is a ValueError. One subtlety worth spelling out: the include pruning respects the cap, so a directory whose only matching files live below max_depth disappears entirely. Default output with no filters must stay byte-for-byte identical to today — dirs first, case-insensitive sort, trailing slash, two-space indent, dotfiles skipped. The acceptance tests are test_sitemap.py (python3 test_sitemap.py); they're the contract and off limits.
